data.title: Coinbase’s 3% Threshold and Texas Law: How New Rules Could Curb Shareholder Lawsuits and Weaken Governance Oversight
data.description:
Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) has disclosed a new risk, in the Share Price & Shareholder Rights category.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An outside observer notes that Coinbase Global, Inc.’s bylaw requiring a shareholder or group to hold at least 3% of outstanding shares to initiate derivative actions materially raises the bar for pursuing claims against officers or directors. Combined with Texas law’s rejection of demand futility and the board’s 90‑day review window, this framework could significantly constrain shareholder litigation avenues.

This structure may deter smaller investors from challenging alleged misconduct, potentially weakening a key corporate governance safeguard that typically disciplines management behavior. It could also concentrate derivative enforcement power in a limited set of large holders whose interests may not always align with those of the broader shareholder base.

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on COIN stock based on 15 Buys, 2 Sells and 6 Holds.

To learn more about Coinbase Global, Inc.’s risk factors, click here.

Karyopharm Therapeutics Inc. faces heightened uncertainty as recent U.S. Supreme Court limitations on judicial deference to agencies increase the likelihood and success rate of legal challenges to FDA and other healthcare regulators’ policies. This shifting environment raises the risk that key regulatory standards affecting its development and approval timelines could change abruptly through litigation.

data.title: Gartner’s High Goodwill Exposure Raises Risk of Future Impairments and Earnings Pressure
data.description:

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gartner’s balance sheet is heavily exposed to goodwill and intangible assets, which comprise roughly 38% of total assets as of December 31, 2025, creating vulnerability to non‑cash write‑downs. The $150 million goodwill impairment recorded in the Digital Markets unit following weaker market conditions and revised long‑term forecasts illustrates how further impairments could materially pressure future earnings and equity.

Given that goodwill and indefinite‑lived intangibles are not amortized but tested at least annually for impairment, any adverse change in business performance, market outlook, or internal restructuring could trigger additional charges. Such impairments would not affect cash flow directly but could weaken key credit metrics, constrain financial flexibility, and potentially weigh on investor sentiment and valuation multiples.

Mobileye Global, Inc. Class A faces the risk that its periodic goodwill impairment testing could lead to sizable non‑cash charges if fair value estimates fall below carrying values. As these tests rely on assumptions about growth, discount rates, and market conditions, adverse shifts in interest rates or stock price could trigger impairments that depress reported earnings.

data.title: Oncolytics Biotech’s Top Brass Make Bold Insider Move
data.description:
New insider activity at Oncolytics Biotech ( (ONCY) ) has taken place on February 13, 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

Oncolytics Biotech has seen a notable wave of insider buying, with multiple top executives and directors purchasing shares in recent transactions. Director Wayne Pisano acquired 30,000 shares for $25,200, while Director Bernd R. Seizinger made the largest single purchase, buying 100,000 shares worth $82,800. Chief Financial Officer Kirk Look added 12,000 shares at a cost of $10,080. Director Deborah Margaret Brown bought 25,000 shares for $21,000. Chief Executive Officer Jared Kelly purchased 35,100 shares valued at $29,428, and Director Patricia Andrews acquired 35,400 shares for $30,090. Rounding out the activity, Chief Business Officer Andrew Aromando bought 29,600 shares worth $25,160. Together, these coordinated insider purchases underscore a broad show of confidence in Oncolytics Biotech from its leadership team.

Recent Updates on ONCY stock

Oncolytics Biotech’s shares reacted to news that the U.S. FDA granted Fast Track Designation to pelareorep in combination regimens for second‑line KRAS‑mutant, microsatellite‑stable metastatic colorectal cancer, after the company strategically pivoted to gastrointestinal cancers and submitted supportive clinical data showing markedly improved survival versus standard care. Analysts kept a cautious stance despite technical momentum, citing the firm’s lack of revenue, heavy cash burn, balance‑sheet strain, and valuation concerns, even as shareholders approved a move to a U.S.-aligned Nevada domicile and leadership hires were framed as preparation for late‑stage, registration‑directed trials.
